layman_headline: Market is bouncing but fear is extreme — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin and the broader crypto market are seeing a short-term bounce after a period of heavy selling. The Fear & Greed index is at 25, which means most investors are scared. While several coins have bounced 5-10% in the past week, the overall trend for altcoins remains bearish — prices are still below their key moving averages. This bounce could easily fade if buyers don't follow through.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines for now. The bounce looks like a relief rally in a larger downtrend. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,850 area and holds, that could become a safer entry point. Don't chase coins that have already jumped 20-100% in a week.
expert_reasoning: All swing candidates present problematic setups. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — a classic sell signal. UB and B have rallied 35% and 125% in a week respectively, making them extended and dangerous to chase. NIGHT is in free fall at -33% weekly with no support structure visible. ETH is the strongest contender with RSI at 39 (our most reliable signal at 83% win rate), Extreme Fear conditions (67% win rate signal), and legitimate ecosystem backing. However, ETH's chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs and only a 1.41x risk/reward to nearest levels. BTC dominance at 56.6% is headwind for all alts. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to retest $1,850 support or for chart bias to improve before committing capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.6% continues to suppress altcoin performance — even strong bounces fade
Extreme Fear (25) can persist or worsen, leading to another leg down before a true bottom
ETH chart structure is bearish with price below all major MAs — this bounce may be a dead cat rally
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,900 resistance — a clean break and hold above could shift chart bias
BTC ability to hold above $65,000 — if it fails, altcoins will sell off harder
Fear & Greed index movement — need to see it rise above 30 to confirm sentiment is turning
market_context: BTC is bouncing near $65.8K supported by 5 consecutive days of ETF inflows, but Extreme Fear at 25 and high BTC dominance at 56.6% suggest this is a relief rally rather than a trend reversal. Altcoins are getting a short-term lift but remain structurally weak.
context_banner: Relief rally in extreme fear — high-quality setups haven't formed yet; disciplined patience protects capital.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25 with flat trend suggests we haven't bottomed yet — historically a contrarian buy zone but requires confirmation, and our 67% win rate with this signal says wait for better alignment.
ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (83% win rate) — but not yet at the 30 threshold that typically marks a strong bounce entry.
Bitcoin ETFs posting 5 straight days of inflows for the first time since April is providing a floor for BTC, which indirectly supports altcoins.
Every candidate with chart data shows bearish structure (below all MAs). UB's Morning Star is the only bullish candle pattern, but the +35% weekly gain makes chasing it risky.
ETH derivatives reset narrative suggests overleveraged positions are being flushed, which could create a cleaner setup for entry once the reset completes.
